recalls.Description:
Positec is recalling several electric chainsaws and pole saws due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected models include the Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded 14" Chainsaw, Worx 8 Amp 14" Electric Chainsaw, Worx 8 Amp Electric Pole Saw, and Portland 9 Amp 14" Chainsaw. Each model features distinct color schemes and branding, which can help consumers identify them. The Hyper Tough model is black with red accents, while the Worx models are orange and black. The Portland chainsaw is green with a black bar.

Consumers should be aware that the main switch on these saws may malfunction, causing them to operate continuously even after the switch has been released, leading to serious injury. Affected products were sold at various retailers, including Walmart and Harbor Freight, as well as online platforms such as Worx.com and Amazon.com between September 2024 and March 2025.
recalls.Title: Positec Recalls Electric Chainsaws and Pole Saws Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Positec at 800-889-0129 from Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or visit https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ for more information.

Products.Name: Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded Chainsaw
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 49,000 (In addition, about 3,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded 14" Chainsaw
Recalled Worx 8 Amp 14" Electric Chainsaw with location of Item No. and Serial No.
Recalled Worx 8 Amp Electric Pole Saw with location of Item No. and Serial No.
Recalled Portland 9 Amp 14" Chainsaw with location of UPC, Serial No. and SKU

Importers.Name: Positec Tool Corporation, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to potential switch failure

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the products and contact Positec for a prepaid shipping label and a replacement saw.

recalls.metaDescription: Positec recalls electric chainsaws and pole saws due to a laceration hazard. Stop using and contact for a replacement.

recalls.Description: DR Power Equipment has issued a recall for its lithium-ion battery-powered chainsaws, specifically models 414170, 414181, 41418, and 414170R. These chainsaws feature a black handle with orange buttons and are labeled with DR Pulse 62V. The model and serial numbers can be found on a white label on the right side of the handle. It is important to note that disposing of the lithium-ion battery in regular trash or recycling is hazardous due to fire risks. Consumers should check with their local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DR Power at 800-940-6923 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@DRPower.com, or visit http://drpower.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,180 (In addition, about 21 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (without battery)
Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (with battery)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of incidents of fire, sparks and/or smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent power equipment retailers across the country and online at https://drpower.com from June 2018 to July 2024, priced between $200 and $350.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Overheating motor controller leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chainsaws and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction is required.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery chainsaws due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for a refund. Learn more here.
